key_insight: The paper establishes parameter-robust stability and solvability for a coupled Darcy--Forchheimer and advection-diffusion-reaction system, then uses that theory to build mixed FEM discretisations and robust preconditioners.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reliable simulation framework for flow-through-porous-media problems where permeability, nonlinear drag, and reaction effects vary widely without degrading numerical stability.
method: Extends perturbed saddle-point stability theory from Hilbert to Banach spaces with parameter-independent bounds.
Applies the abstract result to a mixed formulation of advection-diffusion-reaction coupled with Darcy--Forchheimer flow.
Designs weighted-norm-based operator preconditioners and proves convergence of mixed finite element discretisations in parameter-weighted norms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical stability, convergence, and preconditioner robustness results, but does not mention numerical experiments or datasets.
watch_item: Robust performance depends on correctly chosen weighted norms and compatible mixed finite element spaces, which may complicate implementation and preconditioner design.
paper_title: Parameter-robust well-posedness and discretisation for coupled Darcy--Forchheimer and advection-diffusion-reaction equations
